name: Rat Infestation
description: Hollis, keeper of the General Shop, has a rodent problem in the basement — and perhaps a worse problem than that.
giver_dialogue: Hail, traveler. Thou hast the look of one who can handle trouble, and I am up to my ears in it. I own the General Shop here in Larian. Some months back I let a goblin lodge in my cellar in exchange for a small favor — the details are not important now. He has since let the place fall to ruin. Rats have overrun the basement, and the goblin himself grows bolder by the day. Clear them all out and I shall see thee well rewarded.

steps.description: Clear rats from the General Shop
Hunt the deeper nests in the basement

name: Destroy the Goblin Horde
description: The goblins of the northern stronghold have been raiding Larian's roads. Put an end to them before they put an end to the town.
giver_dialogue: Listen well, friend, for I sing of more than pretty tales these days. The goblin horde has taken the old stronghold to the north, and scarce a week passes without a caravan lost upon the road. If thou hast the steel for it, break their ranks at the source. Larian will remember the deed — and I shall make the reward worth thy while.

steps.description: Break the goblin warbands in the stronghold

name: Wolfman Puck
description: Giant wolves prowl the fields around Larian. Huntsman Puck suspects an orc shaman stirs them up — and wants the beast ended.
giver_dialogue: The realm is overrun with giant wolves, and no natural pack moves like these do. An orc hand guides them, I'd wager my last arrow on it. Thin the packs first — then find the brute pulling their strings. Do us this dastardly favor and I'll keep thy quiver full for a season.

steps.description: Cull the wolf packs in the wilds
Find and slay the orc driving them

name: I Lost My Stuff!
description: Roland ventured too boldly into the Derelict Caves and left his Scroll of Fire behind. He'd very much like it back.
giver_dialogue: Canst thou help a fellow out? I was poking about those ruins to the north — the Derelict Caves — when something big and hungry came up the stairs. I dropped my Scroll of Fire on the way out. Normally I'd fetch it myself, but whatever is down there... well, it wants more firepower than I've got left. Bring it back and it's thine to keep the reward.

name: Destroy the Orc Stronghold
description: A hooded mage suspects that the ruins south of town are no simple cave — they are an orc headquarters, and something darker commands it.
giver_dialogue: Those ruins are no orc warren alone, friend. I feel a colder hand working beneath the stone — a necromancer, unless my bones lie to me. I'll not approach alone, but if thou hast the fire for it, break the orcs and cut the strings of whatever pulls them. I'll see thee armed for the work.

steps.description: Slay the dark mage behind it all

name: Retrieve the Sun Sword
description: Laird the Lord whispers of a blade hidden in a temple on a far island — the Sun Sword, said to carry the light that ancient wyrms fear.
giver_dialogue: I'll be plain with thee, sellsword. There is an old temple called the Citadel of the Sun upon an island far to the east. Within it sleeps a blade forged in daylight — the Sun Sword. Find a boat, brave the deep, and bring it back. If what I fear wakes, this blade may be our only salvation.

steps.description: Retrieve the Sun Sword from the Citadel

name: Slay the Dragon of Asoloth
description: Sage Veylin of Larian has traced the shadow over Asoloth to a single source: an ancient wyrm that slumbers beneath a ruined keep on the far northeastern island. Descend into the lair, end the dragon, and bring its heart to the shrine.
giver_dialogue: Sit, traveler. What I tell thee now leaves this room with thee alone. The unrest in these lands — the wolves, the orcs, the dead that will not lie — they are not the sickness, only its fever. The true blight is an ancient wyrm, older than Larian, older than any scroll I have read. It sleeps beneath a shattered keep on the far northeastern island, and as it stirs the land darkens. Arm thyself well. Take the Sun Sword if thou hast the arm for it. Descend through the broken halls, past the lava that feeds its breath, past the bones of those who tried before. End the wyrm. Cut out its heart and bring it to the shrine — only there may it be undone. Do this, and Asoloth yet has a morning.

steps.description: Slay the Ancient Dragon in its lair
